# EdgeBase Swift SDK

Use this file as a quick-reference contract for AI coding assistants working with the
public Swift SDK packages in this directory.

## Package Boundary

Use the Swift SDK only in trusted app or server code.

The `EdgeBase` package is for client apps and also exposes `EdgeBaseServerClient`
for trusted service-key workflows, while `EdgeBaseCore` holds the shared low-level
primitives.

## Source Of Truth

- Root README: https://github.com/edge-base/edgebase/blob/main/packages/sdk/swift/README.md
- SDK Overview: https://edgebase.fun/docs/sdks
- Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/sdks/client-vs-server
- Admin Users: https://edgebase.fun/docs/authentication/admin-users
- Database Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/database/admin-sdk
- Push Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/push/admin-sdk
- Analytics Admin SDK: https://edgebase.fun/docs/analytics/admin-sdk
- Admin SDK Reference: https://edgebase.fun/docs/admin-sdk/reference

## Public Packages

- `EdgeBaseCore`
- `EdgeBase`
- `EdgeBaseServerClient` type provided by `EdgeBase`

## Canonical Examples

### Create a client

```swift
import EdgeBase

let client = EdgeBaseClient("https://your-project.edgebase.fun")
```

### Query a table

```swift
let posts = try await client.db("shared").table("posts").getList()
```

### Create a service-key client

```swift
let admin = EdgeBaseServerClient("https://your-project.edgebase.fun", serviceKey: "sk-...")
```

### Manage users

```swift
let created = try await admin.adminAuth.createUser(
    email: "june@example.com",
    password: "pass1234"
)
```

### Execute admin actions

```swift
let docs = try await admin.db("workspace", instanceId: "ws-1").table("documents").getList()
```

## Hard Rules

- keep Service Keys out of client apps
- use `EdgeBaseClient` for end-user flows and `EdgeBaseServerClient` for trusted server-side code
- prefer `EdgeBaseCore` for shared low-level primitives
- use async/await for all networked operations
- `EdgeBaseServerClient.destroy()` is async and currently a no-op for symmetry

## Common Mistakes

- do not ship the service-key client into an app bundle
- do not assume synchronous API calls in Swift
- do not bypass `MemoryTokenStorage` in tests when you need deterministic token handling
- do not expose the Service Key through mobile or browser clients

## Quick Reference

```text
EdgeBaseClient(url)                                 -> EdgeBaseClient
EdgeBaseServerClient(url, serviceKey)               -> EdgeBaseServerClient
client.auth                                         -> AuthClient
client.db(namespace, instanceId?)                   -> DbRef
client.storage                                      -> StorageClient
client.functions                                    -> FunctionsClient
client.analytics                                    -> AnalyticsClient
admin.adminAuth                                     -> AdminAuthClient
admin.db(namespace, instanceId?)                   -> AdminDbRef
admin.destroy()                                     -> async Void
EdgeBaseCore                                        -> module / package
```
